spec.reasoning: The task instruction references specific columns by letter (H, I, J) and specific cell addresses (H4, I4, J4), so layout changes like title_row_insert would shift row references and break the task. Instead, I use safe perturbations: (1) Lexical renames - rename the sheet 'Contact List' to 'Client Directory' and rename 'CONTACT LIST' header to 'CLIENT DIRECTORY', plus rename columns on the Data sheet. These test whether the model hardcodes sheet/column names. The instruction references columns by letter (H, I, J) not by name, so renaming the title header is safe. (2) Distractor column - add 'Last Follow-Up Notes' which is plausibly similar to the existing contact-related columns but clearly different, testing whether the model gets confused by extra columns. I avoid title_row_insert because the risk flags indicate header_not_in_row_1 and task_references_specific_cell_addresses, meaning row shifts would break cell references like H4.
